content: Is there any reason to still require invitations to join? (I was just digging in the settings, and it looks like it's easy to set it to public)
Various varieties of spam. I recommend we keep it as it is.
I haven't seen any evidence of spam, and it's a pain to keep having to send out fresh invitations, they expire after 10 days
i mean...
devil's advocate: no evidence of spam could be an argument that invitation links keep the spam away
It's true
I'm a system administrator and I hate having to clean gigabytes of spam out of my servers. I expect any open server to end up being filled with spam.
But I give an invite link to anyone who asks for it, the original one was seen by 10s of thousands of people, etc
this is also true
My final point before resting my case: I think it's also easy to switch it back to private later
I was on Hacker News and Reddit yesterday and I thought about letting people know about the CT Server. Any thoughts?
aaaaaaaaa
please don't
hacker news culture is
not something i want to bring here

Yes, please don't massively advertise this place to people who don't know category theory. And especially not if we take the crazy step of not requiring invites. Saying "let's not require invited - I haven't seen spam here yet" is a bit like saying "coronavirus deaths are dropping so let's drop social distancing".
Requiring invites means that people need to be willing to put 1 minute of work into getting here. That's a pretty nice way to filter out people without a real interest in category theory.

Based on recent comments, it seems we hadn't considered the inconvenience for the people who have to generate invite links, and this is an obstacle to anyone encouraging a class of their students to get on board, say. Maybe we could re-open this discussion?

[Mod] Morgan Rogers said:

encouraging a class of their students to get on board

There's a slightly different argument on whether we want to be that open
Encouraging (which to a student might not be distinguishable from ordering) hoards of students to join is a step further than merely being public
Does Zulip support bots?
The easiest course of action would be to set up a bot that generates a link every 10 days in a dedicated stream.
Yes, Zulip supports bots that respond to messages, so users could request an invite link.
If we could make it possible to do that in private messages, that looks at first sight to be an ideal solution, since it's a little annoying for the requests to be public messages every time.
Bots can respond to private messages, yes.
fyi, it is possible for anyone to invite people image.png
it's just that you need to give an email address to send the invite to image.png
If there's a friendly hacker who could knock together a bot that responds to a command like \invitelinkplz it might be a perfect solution...
Perhaps one already exists in the ecosystem
What's the functional difference from having a link every 10 days and going open?
I'm scared of flooding the chat too, though honestly I think it's worth (and fair) trying to open for a while, and see how it goes
Regarding the bot, I think an easier option is for someone to do it manually every 9 days. I can easily be that someone.

I guess the functional difference is that each individual invite link self destructs after 10 days, so if someone posts one on /b/ and says "raid this" then we only get 10 days of headaches instead of forever
Whereas if we go public then there is a sign up link (with a plain english url instead of the generated one) that stays alive forever
It can't be turned off once it's on?
if you scroll back up, i think the functional difference was more like "it creates slightly more friction, which means that you only get people signing up who have slightly more motivation to do so"
or at least, some have argued that this is true...
Could we give the public read-only access, so that anyone can read the discussions but they need an account to post anything?

Jules Hedges said:

I guess the functional difference is that each individual invite link self destructs after 10 days, so if someone posts one on /b/ and says "raid this" then we only get 10 days of headaches instead of forever

Uh I was quite convinced it could be switched off at will. Let me check.

sarahzrf said:

if you scroll back up, i think the functional difference was more like "it creates slightly more friction, which means that you only get people signing up who have slightly more motivation to do so"

It makes sense, but on the side of who publishes the link. For new users the hassle should be the same in both cases?
But I have a sense I'm missing some important detail of how going public works

Here: https://categorytheory.zulipchat.com/help/roles-and-permissions
there's an interesting remark:

Some organization settings allow an organization to restrict the permissions of New Members; Members who do not have those restrictions are called Full Members.
This could be a nice safeguard against an invasion

Matteo Capucci said:

Uh I was quite convinced it could be switched off at will. Let me check.

Looking at settings I'm fairly sure one can switch it on and off at will
I'll just say I'm willing to let people experiment with things here...

Matteo Capucci said:

Regarding the bot, I think an easier option is for someone to do it manually every 9 days. I can easily be that someone.

The difference is that if we get flooded, we stop generating links and we'll know that in 10 days the nightmare will end.

Having read-only public access is not just needed, I think it's fundamental. I have already a couple of works in progress where I use links to conversations happened here as references. Given the amount of participants on this platform and how lively it is, I'm quite sure that a boatload of work in the future will originate from discussions happening here.

Fabrizio Genovese said:

Matteo Capucci said:

Regarding the bot, I think an easier option is for someone to do it manually every 9 days. I can easily be that someone.

The difference is that if we get flooded, we stop generating links and we'll know that in 10 days the nightmare will end.

I'm not sure why you quoted that message, but in any case I see the point. I pointed out above this works with open access too--it's literally changing an option from a dropdown. So bad things should happen, we turn it off instantly which is even better imo

Fabrizio Genovese said:

Having read-only public access is not just needed, I think it's fundamental. I have already a couple of works in progress where I use links to conversations happened here as references. Given the amount of participants on this platform and how lively it is, I'm quite sure that a boatload of work in the future will originate from discussions happening here.

+1 for this, but I can't find an option in the organization settings! :fear:
Btw, what about a nice poll about going public? To avoid this discussion to stagnate and end in nothing. At least the @moderators will have a mandate to change the settings.
When I generate an invite link, I have the option for the invited party to be added "as Guest", which I presume is like a read only option?

Matteo Capucci said:

Btw, what about a nice poll about going public? To avoid this discussion to stagnate and end in nothing. At least the @moderators will have a mandate to change the settings.

I'm sure it's possible to make a poll, at least on our work zulip we have polls to decide what day to drink beer on zoom. But I can't figure out how to do it...
I found instructions
I would propose to run the poll here in order to get the opinion of those who are interested enough to be on the meta channel
It's still 437 people, in theory
That feeling when we do things here with a better system than the brexit referendum
Thanks @[Mod] Morgan Rogers !

I don't want to add yet another option, but I feel compelled to reiterate that there's a middle ground between guest and full member which is 'new member', i.e. Zulip can restrict new members for a given time after they register.
Are we confident that option 3 (create a bot), currently nearly tied for most popular, is actually feasible?
I volunteered to impersonate such bot, though not through private messages: I could just post a new link to the aptly named 'invite link' topic every ~10 days, so ppl always have a fresh one ready

Jules Hedges said:

Are we confident that option 3 (create a bot), currently nearly tied for most popular, is actually feasible?

It seems like a Generic bot should have the power to do this, so yes.

[Mod] Morgan Rogers said:

/poll Should Zulip go public? That is, should we:
Keep things how they are
Create a bot that one can message privately to receive a read only invite link
Create a bot that one can message privately to receive an ordinary invite link
Go completely public (no restrictions to becoming an ordinary member)
Go "read-only" public: allow anyone to view, but require one of the above options to become an ordinary member

Lol, I wanted to vote but I ended up adding another thing T_T
Oops... It seems that added options are hard to remove (I can only edit the original options), so hopefully everyone will just ignore that :sweat_smile:
Clearly every election should be like this: Anyone should be able to add any new option while the vote is ongoing
It's not really any weirder than seeing Theresa May standing next to Lord Buckethead during the counting of the last general election

Jules Hedges said:

Clearly every election should be like this: Anyone should be able to add any new option while the vote is ongoing

While the vote is ongoing? I'm not fan of changing the playing field during the game
In the end I decided to take responsibility and voted (also) for the bs option I introduced
approval voting
i approve

It seems like the bot option is in the lead. If such a bot were created, would there be some way to control which streams users are subscribed to when they join? I feel like by default it should be all of them, because it's easy to unsubscribe from a stream but really hard to discover a stream exists if you're not already subscribed to it.

I agree, @Nathaniel Virgo - everyone should be subscribed to anything, since there's no way to tell what people are interested in, and if they're not subscribed they may never find out about the streams they're interested in. It's easy to unsubscribe from streams.
Another question that would fit the title of this thread: should zulipchat be world readable, so that people can link to discussions here from other websites?
This feels like a silly question but how can I vote? Clicking the option I want doesn't seem to be working...
You click on the box with the number in it
When I do this the box gains a black outline, but if I navigate away and come back the change is gone, so I don't think it's permanent?
Mmmh I see your vote
There's a list of people who voted next to each options and I see your name
Ah I see my vote now too! I had to restart the client!

Henry Story said:

Another question that would fit the title of this thread: should zulipchat be world readable, so that people can link to discussions here from other websites?

That's the "read-only" public option, option 5.

How does one vote?
There is documentation on how to create a poll but it does not say how to vote. Does one enter a number into the box and click "Add Choice"? If so does the numbering start at 0 or 1? (or even -2 for HoTT folks)
Click on the box next to the options you want to select. Please do not add choices! It seems you have already voted for options 1, 3 and 5, @Henry Story
Ah ok, it did not seem to make any changes when I clicked on the buttons. I think I mistakenly clicked on 1 then.
Ah I see one can unvote by re-clicking, and then for me in Safari: reloading the page.

[Mod] Morgan Rogers said:

Jules Hedges said:

Are we confident that option 3 (create a bot), currently nearly tied for most popular, is actually feasible?

It seems like a Generic bot should have the power to do this, so yes.

Now that I'm actually attempting to do this, I realise that I don't have the online programming chops to do it myself. I don't even know how to clone a git repository :upside_down: if someone with some experience can help me with the basics, I should be able to write the actual code once the set-up is there, since the bot API is all in Python.
I can help but I think we need access to this instance which has been created by @Christian Williams if I recall
We can schedule a call and try to set it up
Sounds good to me, maybe early next week?
:thumbs_up:
